title: End-to-End Release, Migration, Recovery, and TypeScript Engineering Defense · Contract Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for end-to-end release, migration, recovery, and typescript engineering defense and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, compile diagnostics, explicit non-claims, and named browser, TypeScript, Node, network, filesystem, terminal, load, accessibility, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for defensible recoverable TypeScript Pokedex release dossier. Learners progress thro…
objectives: Bind source, lockfile, compiler, tests, emitted JavaScript, packed artifact, command behavior, saved data, checksums, and stakeholder review to one revision.
Execute invalid input, not found, HTTP failure, malformed JSON, stale Promise, abort, cache corruption, write interruption, signal, and listener-leak cases.
Prove schema migration, old-package compatibility limits, backup, rollback, restored data identity, and support decisions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using record and remaining plus specific case evidence.
